title: Paychex, Inc. (PAYX) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Paychex, Inc. (PAYX) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 5.5% growth rate reflects steady, mid-single-digit expansion. The core payroll business grows slowly with the broader economy, while higher-margin services like Professional Employer Organization (PEO) and HR outsourcing provide incremental growth, balanced against intensifying competition from modern, cloud-native HR platforms.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 7.5% discount rate highlights Paychex's deeply entrenched moat, exceptionally high client retention (typically over 80%), and strong balance sheet. The predictability of its recurring revenue streams merits a lower risk premium relative to the broader market.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term inflation and broader GDP growth expectations. Given the maturity of the U.S. payroll processing market, it is unlikely the business will persistently outpace the overall economy in perpetuity.

thesisParagraphs: Paychex benefits from an extraordinarily sticky business model driven by compliance complexity and high switching costs. For small and medium-sized businesses, managing payroll tax calculations, benefits administration, and ever-changing labor regulations is a massive headache. By outsourcing this to Paychex, businesses shift the regulatory risk and administrative burden. Because the cost of switching providers is high—risking payroll errors and employee dissatisfaction—retention rates are exceptional, creating a reliable, annuity-like stream of cash flows.

Furthermore, the company's financial model is robust. Not only does it generate significant fee income, but it also earns interest on the "float"—the billions of dollars of client funds it holds in transit before they are distributed to employees or tax authorities. This creates a natural hedge, allowing Paychex to benefit from higher interest rate environments. The current valuation suggests the market accurately prices in this high-quality, wide-moat, slow-growth cash engine.
verdictFooter: Based on current market data and estimates. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini project a 5.5% FCF growth rate?
faq.answer: The 5.5% projection balances the reliable, recession-resistant nature of core payroll processing with the faster-growing, higher-margin Human Capital Management (HCM) and PEO segments. It also accounts for the maturity of the core market and competition from agile tech startups.

faq.question: What is the 'float' and why does it matter?
faq.answer: The 'float' refers to the client funds Paychex holds temporarily between when an employer runs payroll and when the funds clear employee accounts or tax agencies. Paychex invests this cash in short-term securities, generating substantial interest income. When interest rates rise, this high-margin revenue stream directly boosts the bottom line.

faq.question: Why is the verdict 'Fair Value'?
faq.answer: At the current price, the model indicates that Paychex is trading very close to its intrinsic value. The market is efficiently pricing in the company's defensive characteristics, reliable dividend, and consistent cash flow generation, leaving a narrow margin of safety.
